The '''Stellan''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stellan (planet)|Stellan]]. A fiercely isolationist but peaceful race, the Stellan maintained that tranquility by [[Ter-Stellan|abandoning]] [[Youngling/Legends|children]] that they identified as too aggressive. Those children, such as [[Jeigh Esse]], were launched into [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. Esse herself was discovered by [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]] and followed in their trade as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Stellan were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] in form. Their face was dominated by a large [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], filled with white, square [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Two black protrusions extended from their jaws, curving to a point. They had black, pupil-less [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], which appeared as two narrow wedges, and stood out in stark contrast to their white skin pigmentation. The Stellan [[Jeigh Esse]] had three black shapes growing from the top of her head; one on each side by her small [[Ear/Legends|ears]], and one on the very top of her head. The Stellan had four-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]] and an opposable thumb, with which they could manipulate items.<ref name="SWG7" />

==Society and culture==
The Stellan had a largely peaceful civilization, and rejected any members of the species that were overly aggressive. All Stellan children were screened at an early age, and if any were found to be mentally ill, violent, or a threat to the Stellan's way of life, they were dubbed "[[ter-Stellan]]" and launched into [[Realspace/Legends|space]], at the mercy of whoever might stumble across the abandoned children. This practice stemmed from the Stellan belief of the "[[Vain Goddess]]," who had provoked the galaxy into an unending war as an offering of love and worship. The Stellan feared that if they succumbed to the Vain Goddess's song, the soul of Stellan would be lost forever. Stellan society was fiercely isolationist, and had a theocratic government ruled by a [[priest-king]].<ref name="TNMS">{{SW|url=news/the-not-so-magnificent-seven|text=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|int=The Not-So Magnificent Seven|nolive=1}}</ref>

==History==
The Stellan evolved on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stellan (planet)|Stellan]], located in the north-western corner of the [[Hunnoverrs sector/Legends|Hunnoverrs sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="Atlas" /> As the Stellan practice of exiling children was discovered, many [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] and [[Slavery/Legends|slavers]] from the nearby worlds of [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and [[Rodia/Legends|Rodia]] began to make trips to Stellan's [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] to prey on the discarded Stellan. One Stellan child that was identified as being too aggressive was Jeigh Esse. Esse was infamous on Stellan as one of the most vicious and conniving of the ter-Stellan—Esse framed countless other Stellan for her misdeeds, leading many innocent Stellan children to be exiled.<ref name="TNMS" /> She was eventually launched into space and discovered by a group of [[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]]<ref name="SWG7" />—the traditional Rodian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of Rodia being only two [[Sector/Legends|sectors]] galactic west.<ref name="Atlas" /> They raised her as one of their own, instructing her in their craft as [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunters]]. Esse became a lethal hunter. Among her diverse weaponry was a [[polarizer]] that she adapted from a [[BlasTech Industries/Legends|BlasTech]] [[EG-7 blaster]].<ref name="SWG7" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Stellan were created by [[Chris Cowdrill]], a fifteen-year-old at the time, as part of his entry in the [[Design An Alien]] contest held in the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]s'' in [[1995]]. Cowdrill gained second place in the under-16 category, and his entry was published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] in [[1996]]. [[Leland Chee]], Keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database|Holocron]], has since stated that the published "Design an Alien" entries are considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the "Design an Alien" contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref> ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' acknowledged the Stellan by including their homeworld in its appendix. The [[StarWars.com]] blog ''[[The Not-So Magnificent Seven]]'', written by [[Greg Mitchell]], provides more insight into Stellan society, as well as establishing the plural of the species as "Stellan", rather than "Stellans".<ref name="TNMS" />
